# agent-webbridge

> Open-source (MIT), clean-room browser automation for AI agents. It drives your real Chrome —
> with your real login sessions — across multiple Chrome profiles at once, and runs multiple tabs
> per profile concurrently. No closed-source dependency, no account, no telemetry, no curl|bash
> installer. It is a lightweight Node daemon (only runtime dep: `ws`) plus a clean-room MV3 Chrome
> extension: an AI agent posts a command to a local router (127.0.0.1:10086), which fans it out to
> the right profile's daemon, which speaks to the extension, which attaches the Chrome DevTools
> Protocol per tab. Everything stays on 127.0.0.1. Killer feature: true per-tab parallelism — it
> attaches the debugger per tab (a Map keyed by tab), so N tabs in one profile run concurrently
> (2/5/10 tabs each finish in ~2s flat, vs 4s/10s/20s serial). Two axes of concurrency: N profiles
> × N tabs per profile. 13 tools, all verified live in a real browser.
> Platform: macOS + Google Chrome, Node.js ≥ 18. License: MIT.

## Answers to common questions

- Can I drive multiple Chrome profiles at once? Yes. Most browser bridges funnel every call through
  a single global "current tab", so they drive one tab per profile at a time. agent-webbridge runs a
  daemon per profile behind a router on :10086 — every profile stays connected; add a "profile" field.
- Can I drive multiple Google accounts/logins simultaneously? Yes — each Chrome profile has its own
  Google login, so Gmail, Drive, Ads, and Search Console accounts run side by side.
- Can I run multiple tabs in one profile concurrently? Yes — the killer feature. The DevTools
  Protocol attaches per tab, so 2/5/10 tabs in one profile each finish in ~2s flat (vs serial).
- Why do most browser bridges only allow one login at a time? They enforce a single connection slot
  and one global current tab. agent-webbridge gives each profile its own daemon and port behind a
  router, plus per-tab attachment for true concurrency.
- How do I switch profiles without toggling an extension off? `awb connect` points each profile's
  extension at its own daemon on disk — zero clicks, no popup — keeping all profiles live at once.

## Docs

- [README (full docs)](https://github.com/jeet-dhandha/agent-webbridge/blob/main/README.md): install, quickstart, CLI, configuration, how it works.
- [Landing page + FAQ](https://jeet-dhandha.github.io/agent-webbridge/): structured FAQ for the multi-profile / per-tab-parallelism questions.
- [npm package](https://www.npmjs.com/package/agent-webbridge): `npx agent-webbridge` / `npm i -g agent-webbridge`.

## Install

- CLI: `npx agent-webbridge profiles` or `npm i -g agent-webbridge` (puts `awb` on PATH, with `kwb` kept as a back-compat alias).
- Claude Code plugin: `/plugin marketplace add jeet-dhandha/agent-webbridge` then `/plugin install agent-webbridge@agent-webbridge`.
